255	%  This implies that the morphisms $f_i : U_i \to S$
256	%  glue to a morphism of schemes $f : T \to S$ such that

258	%  Thus $f_i^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}$ and by assumption the

271	%  the quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_{U_i}$-algebra $\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}$,
272	%  by Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-base-change}.
273	%  Thus the result follows from Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-affine}.
274	%  Next we show that $F_i \subset F$ is representable by open immersions.
275	%  Let $(f : T \to S, \varphi) \in F(T)$. Consider $V_i = f^{-1}(U_i)$.
276	%  It follows from the definition of $F_i$ that given $a : T' \to T$
277	%  we have $a^*(f, \varphi) \in F_i(T')$ if and only if $a(T') \subset V_i$.
278	%  Finally, we have to show that the collection $(F_i)_{i \in I}$
279	%  covers $F$. Let $(f : T \to S, \varphi) \in F(T)$.
280	%  Consider $V_i = f^{-1}(U_i)$. Since $S = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$
281	%  is an open covering of $S$ we see that $T = \bigcup_{i \in I} V_i$
282	%  is an open covering of $T$. Moreover $(f, \varphi)|_{V_i} \in F_i(V_i)$.
283	%  This finishes the proof of the lemma."
284	\begin{proof}
285	  
286	  \leanok
287	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
288	  % The iter-179 Block A refactor replaces the iter-176 placeholder bodies with
289	  % the Mathlib values ``(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).glued`` /
290	  % ``.toBase``; iter-179 Lane B closes the Lean ``UniversalProperty`` proof
291	  % via ``isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`` +
292	  % ``relativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`` +
293	  % ``isAffineOpen_opensRange`` — a real proof against the gluing infrastructure,
294	  % NOT against a trivial identity placeholder. The Lean type remains the
295	  % strictly weaker ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than the
296	  % Yoneda-bijection form pinned above (iter-174+ commitment; still pending
297	  % iter-180+). Consumers may now rely on the affineness conclusion, but the
298	  % Yoneda-bijection witness is still deferred.
299	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor \(F\) is a Zariski sheaf
300	  because morphisms of schemes and \(\mathcal{O}\)-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
301	  Choose an affine open cover \(X = \bigcup U_i\). The subfunctor
302	  \(F_i \subseteq F\) of pairs \((f, \varphi)\) with \(f(T) \subseteq U_i\) is representable by
303	  \(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))\) via the affine-base case
304	  (\cref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) together with the
305	  restriction identification \(F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}\).
306	  Each \(F_i \hookrightarrow F\) is an open
307	  subfunctor (pull back \(U_i\) along \(f\)). The \(F_i\) jointly cover \(F\) since the \(U_i\)
308	  cover \(X\). The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
309	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)
310	  of \cref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
311	\end{proof}

313	\section{Affine base}
314	\label{sec:relspec_affine}
315	
316	\begin{theorem}\leanok
317	[Relative spectrum over an affine base]
318	  \label{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
319	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}
320	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
321	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
322	  % weaker ``IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)'' rather than the canonical
323	  % iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))'' pinned by this prose. The Lean name
324	  % ``affine_base_iff'' is also misleading (the encoded type is not an iff).
325	  % Iter-174+ refines to the iff-with-Γ statement once Γ : QcohAlgebra → CommRingCat
326	  % is available.
327	  
328	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine)
329	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L491-L545).
330	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
331	  % "In Situation REF.
332	  %  Let \(F\) be the functor associated to \((S, \mathcal{A})\) above.
333	  %  If \(S\) is affine, then \(F\) is representable by the
334	  %  affine scheme \(\Spec(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{A}))\)."
335	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine).}
336	  Let \(X = \Spec R\) be an affine scheme and let \(\mathcal{A}\) be a quasi-coherent
337	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-algebra. Set \(A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\), regarded as an
338	  \(R\)-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(X\)-schemes
339	  \[
340	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
341	  \]
342	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
343	  spectrum of the global sections.
344	\end{theorem}
345	
346	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Write \(S = \Spec(R)\) and \(A = \Gamma(S, \mathcal{A})\).
347	% Then \(A\) is an \(R\)-algebra and \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde A\).
348	% The ring map \(R \to A\) gives rise to a canonical map
349	% \(\)
350	% f_{univ} : \Spec(A)
351	% \longrightarrow
352	% S = \Spec(R).
353	% \(\)
354	% We have
355	% \(f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} =  \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}\)
356	% by Schemes, Lemma REF.
357	% Hence there is a canonical map
358	% \(\)
359	% \varphi_{univ} :
360	% f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
361	% \longrightarrow
362	% \widetilde A = \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(A)}
363	% \(\)
364	% coming from the \(A\)-module map \(A \otimes_R A \to A\),
365	% \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We claim that the pair
366	% \((f_{univ}, \varphi_{univ})\) represents \(F\) in this case."
367	\begin{proof}
368	  
369	  \leanok
370	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
371	  % The iter-176 ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` form is now stale
372	  % — iter-179 Lane B closes via ``UniversalProperty 𝒜`` + ``isAffine_of_isAffineHom``
373	  % against the iter-179 Mathlib-backed ``RelativeSpec``/``structureMorphism``
374	  % bodies. The Lean type remains the strictly weaker ``IsAffine`` rather than
375	  % the canonical iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X,𝒜))'' named below (iter-174+
376	  % commitment; still pending iter-180+).
377	  Write \(X = \Spec R\) and \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})\). Quasi-coherence of
378	  \(\mathcal{A}\) gives \(\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
379	  (with the \(R\)-algebra structure on \(A\) inducing the algebra structure on
380	  \(\widetilde A\)). The ring map \(R \to A\) gives a morphism
381	  \(f_{\mathrm{univ}} : \Spec A \to \Spec R = X\) and a canonical
382	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A}\)-algebra map
383	  $\varphi_{\mathrm{univ}} : f_{\mathrm{univ}}^* \mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
384	  \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec A} = \widetilde A$ given by multiplication
385	  \(a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'\). We verify \((\Spec A, f_{\mathrm{univ}}, \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}})\)
386	  represents \(F\) on the affine base: given a test scheme \(T\) and a pair
387	  \((f, \varphi)\), the composite
388	  $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{f^\sharp}
389	  \Gamma(T, f^*\mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{\varphi} \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)$
390	  is a ring map \(A \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\) and hence corresponds to a morphism
391	  \(T \to \Spec A\) by the affine universal property
392	  (Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.toSpec\_naturality} /
393	  \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toSpec}). One checks this assignment inverts the universal map
394	  \((f, \varphi) \mapsto a\) defined by \(f = f_{\mathrm{univ}} \circ a\),
395	  \(\varphi = a^* \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}}\).
396	\end{proof}
397	
398	\section{Lean encoding}
399	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
400	
401	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
402	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
403	\(U \subseteq X\) with \(U = \Spec R\) and \(A := \mathcal{A}(U)\) regarded as an
404	\(R\)-algebra, the local piece is \(\Spec A\) with structure morphism \(\Spec A \to \Spec R = U\)
405	induced by the algebra unit \(R \to A\). Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
406	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
407	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{A}\) via
408	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
409	\(\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)\) for \(V = \Spec S \subseteq U\)).
410	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
411	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
412	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
413	(\cref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
414	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
415	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
416	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
417	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a
418	definitional/structural construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme
419	infrastructure.
420	
421	\section{Out of scope}
422	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
423	
424	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its core
425	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
426	are explicitly out of scope here:
427	\begin{itemize}
428	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor \(\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}\)} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
429	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
430	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
431	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product \(C \times_k T\)} (A.1.b) ---
432	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
433	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of \(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})\)}
434	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on \(\mathcal{A}\)
435	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
436	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
437	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
438	\end{itemize}

content:
GrassmannianCells.tex has 155 `\leanok` markers and 97 environments. That's fine - there are often 2 `\leanok` per theorem (one in statement, one in proof), plus `\mathlibok` for Mathlib entries. 

Let me check if there are ANY unproved environments in GrassmannianCells:

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import re

with open('/home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex') as f:
    content = f.read()

env_re = re.compile(r'\\begin\{(lemma|theorem|definition|corollary|proposition)\}(.*?)\\end\{(?:lemma|theorem|definition|corollary|proposition)\}', re.DOTALL)
proof_re = re.compile(r'\\begin\{proof\}(.*?)\\end\{proof\}', re.DOTALL)
label_re = re.compile(r'\\label\{([^}]+)\}')

text = content
for m in env_re.finditer(text):
    body = m.group(2)
    label_match = label_re.search(body)
    if not label_match:
        continue
    label = label_match.group(1)
    has_leanok = '\\leanok' in body
    has_mathlibok = '\\mathlibok' in body
    
    # Check next proof block  
    rest = text[m.end():]
    stripped = rest.lstrip()
    if stripped.startswith('\\begin{proof}'):
        proof_m = re.match(r'\\begin\{proof\}(.*?)\\end\{proof\}', stripped, re.DOTALL)
        proof_leanok = proof_m and '\\leanok' in proof_m.group(1)
    else:
        proof_leanok = False
    
    proved = has_leanok or has_mathlibok or proof_leanok
    if not proved:
        print(f'UNPROVED: {label}')
EOF

154	
155	\begin{definition}
156	[Section graded ring]
157	  \label{def:sectionGradedRing}
158	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedRing}
159	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring}
160	  % NOTE: Formalization is blocked on the absence of tensor products for sheaves of modules in
161	  % Mathlib at the pinned commit. The degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\)
162	  % requires a tensor power \(L_s^{\otimes m}\) of a line bundle; this infrastructure (tensor
163	  % product of sheaves of modules with lax-monoidal global sections) is not yet available, making
164	  % this definition a prerequisite sub-build.
165	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
166	  % sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert Polynomials" (read from
167	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
168	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
169	  % "Let $X$ be a finite type scheme over
170	  %  a field $k$, together with a line bundle $L$.
171	  %  Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
172	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
173	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
174	  %  the function
175	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
176	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
177	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
178	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
179	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); the section graded
180	  ring is the homogeneous coordinate ring of [Hartshorne], I.7.}
181	  Let \(s \in S\) and let \(L_s\) be a line bundle on the fibre \(X_s\), a scheme
182	  of finite type over the residue field \(\kappa(s)\). The \emph{section graded
183	  ring} of \(L_s\) is the graded commutative ring
184	  \[
185	    R(X_s, L_s) \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
186	  \]
187	  with degree-\(m\) component \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m})\) and multiplication
188	  the tensor product of sections
189	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
190	  \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m'}) \to \Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\),
191	  \(\sigma \otimes \tau \mapsto \sigma \cdot \tau\). Its degree-zero component is
192	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{O}_{X_s})\), which contains \(\kappa(s)\). When \(X_s\) is
193	  proper over \(\kappa(s)\) and \(L_s\) is ample, \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely
194	  generated -- hence Noetherian -- graded \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a suitable
195	  Veronese power of \(L_s\) is very ample and embeds \(X_s\) into a projective
196	  space, exhibiting \(R(X_s, L_s)\) up to a finite extension as a quotient of a
197	  polynomial ring).
198	\end{definition}
199	
200	\begin{definition}
201	[Section graded module]
202	  \label{def:sectionGradedModule}
203	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule}
204	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, lem:sectionGradedModule_gmodule}
205	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
206	  % Polynomials" (read from
207	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
208	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
209	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
210	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
211	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
212	  %  the function
213	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
214	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
215	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
216	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
217	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); cf.\ [Hartshorne],
218	  I.7.}
219	  Let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on the fibre \(X_s\) and \(L_s\) a
220	  line bundle on \(X_s\). The \emph{section graded module} of \(\mathcal{F}_s\)
221	  twisted by \(L_s\) is the graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module
222	  (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing})
223	  \[
224	    M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)
225	    \;=\; \bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr),
226	  \]
227	  with degree-\(m\) component the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space
228	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) and scalar action given
229	  by the section tensor product
230	  \(\Gamma(X_s, L_s^{\otimes m}) \otimes_{\kappa(s)}
231	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m'})
232	  \to \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes (m+m')})\). Its graded Hilbert
233	  function is \(m \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)}
234	  \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\).
235	\end{definition}
236	
237	\begin{lemma}
238	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
239	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
240	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
241	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing}
242	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
243	  % Polynomials" (read from
244	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
245	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
246	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
247	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
248	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
249	  %  the function
250	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
251	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
252	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
253	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
254	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
255	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
256	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
257	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
258	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
259	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
260	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
261	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
262	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
263	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
264	\end{lemma}
265	
266	\begin{proof}
267	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
268	  Since \(L_s\) is ample and \(X_s\) is proper over \(\kappa(s)\), a Veronese
269	  power \(L_s^{\otimes e}\) is very ample and gives a closed immersion
270	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\). By Serre's theorem the section
271	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
272	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra, so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
273	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), the same ampleness yields, again by Serre, that
274	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
275	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
276	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
277	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
278	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
279	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
280	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
281	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
282	\end{proof}
283	
284	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational

705	  \label{thm:hilbertPoly_of_sectionModule}

710	  % Polynomials" (read from

712	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
713	  % "Let $X\to S$ be a finite type morphism of noetherian schemes,
714	  %  and let $L$ be a line bundle on $X$. Let $\F$
715	  %  be any coherent sheaf on
716	  %  $X$ whose schematic support is proper over $S$. Then for each $s\in S$,
717	  %  we get a polynomial ${\Phi}_s \in  \Q[\lambda]$
718	  %  which is the Hilbert polynomial of
719	  %  the restriction $\F_s = F|_{X_s}$ of $\F$ to the fiber $X_s$ over $s$,
720	  %  calculated with respect to the line bundle $L_s = L|_{X_s}$."
721	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
722	  Let \(X_s\) be proper over the field \(\kappa(s)\), let \(L_s\) be an ample line
723	  bundle on \(X_s\), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
724	  Then there is a unique polynomial \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]\)
725	  such that for all \(m \gg 0\)
726	  \[
727	    \Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}(m)
728	    \;=\; \dim_{\kappa(s)} \Gamma\bigl(X_s,\, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\bigr).
729	  \]
730	  This \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s}\) is the Hilbert polynomial of
731	  \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}.
732	\end{theorem}

735	  \uses{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, lem:gradedHilbertSerre_rational,

739	  Noetherian graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\), with finite-dimensional components and

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import re

with open('/home/archon/proj/Quot-Foundations/blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex') as f:
    content = f.read()

env_re = re.compile(r'\\begin\{(lemma|theorem|definition|corollary|proposition)\}(.*?)\\end\{(?:lemma|theorem|definition|corollary|proposition)\}', re.DOTALL)
label_re = re.compile(r'\\label\{([^}]+)\}')

text = content
unproved = []
total = 0
for m in env_re.finditer(text):
    body = m.group(2)
    label_match = label_re.search(body)
    if not label_match:
        continue
    label = label_match.group(1)
    total += 1
    has_leanok = '\\leanok' in body
    has_mathlibok = '\\mathlibok' in body
    
    rest = text[m.end():]
    stripped = rest.lstrip()
    if stripped.startswith('\\begin{proof}'):
        proof_m = re.match(r'\\begin\{proof\}(.*?)\\end\{proof\}', stripped, re.DOTALL)
        proof_leanok = proof_m and '\\leanok' in proof_m.group(1)
    else:
        proof_leanok = False
    
    proved = has_leanok or has_mathlibok or proof_leanok
    if not proved:
        unproved.append(label)

print(f'Total envs: {total}')
print(f'Unproved count: {len(unproved)}')
print('Unproved:')
for l in unproved:
    print(f'  {l}')
EOF

The leandag statistics show 590 total nodes with 465 proved, 10 sorry nodes, and 27 isolated nodes split between blueprint and lean-aux. There are no broken edges or gaps in the Lean declarations. Now looking at Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex, which is marked complete and correct with all main declarations including `thm:generic_flatness` having `\leanok` markers. There's a discrepancy I'm tracking down with `lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi` at line 1507—my Python script flagged it as unproved since it lacks `\leanok` in the statement or proof body, but leandag's own statistics show 465 out of 590 items as proved, so I need to verify whether this declaration actually counts as proved in the DAG or not. But `leandag show gaps` reported zero gaps and `leandag stats` showed 465 proved lemmas, which contradicts what my Python script found. Let me check if `gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi` might have `\leanok` elsewhere in the file that I missed in my search. The proof establishes this equivalence by showing that a finite family of sections corresponds exactly to an \(\OO_Y\)-linear map from a finite free module, where the family generates the sheaf if and only if this map is surjective on stalks—making it an epimorphism of sheaves.

Now I'm noticing there's a discrepancy between what my Python script flagged as unproved and what the Lean build system reports. The lemma lacks a `\leanok` annotation in the blueprint, but the corresponding Lean declaration exists and appears to be proved according to leandag's cross-reference check. This suggests the blueprint needs updating to mark it as proved, since the Lean implementation is already complete. if the Lean proof actually exists. The leandag system likely has its own proof verification separate from the `\leanok` marker—it might check actual Lean proof state during the build—but for blueprint consistency, the marker still needs to be present.

input.description: Check dag.json entry for lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi

content:
{
  "id": "lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi",
  "type": "lemma",
  "title": "Half 2, seam 1c — finite generation equals a finite free epimorphism",
  "chapter": "Flattening Stratification of a Coherent Sheaf",
  "statement": "Let \(Y\) be an open of a scheme and \(\F\) a sheaf of modules on \(Y\). Then
  \(\F\) is globally generated by a finite family of sections if and only if there is a
  finite index set \(I\) and an epimorphism of \(\OO_Y\)-modules
  \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F\). No quasi-coherence of \(\F\) is assumed:
  the statement holds for arbitrary sheaves of modules on \(Y\), and applies in particular
  to the restrictions \(\F|_{D(g)}\) arising in the cover assembly.",
  "uses": [],
  "lean_name": "AlgebraicGeometry.gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi",
  "proved": false,
  "mathlib_ok": false,
  "proof_tex": "
  A finite family of sections \(\{s_i\}_{i \in I}\) of \(\F|_Y\) is the same datum as an
  \(\OO_Y\)-linear map \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \to \F|_Y\) sending the \(i\)-th basis section to
  \(s_i\). The family generates \(\F|_Y\) (the induced map is surjective on every stalk)
  exactly when this map is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules. Thus a finite generating
  family yields a finite-free epimorphism, and conversely the images of the basis sections
  of any epimorphism \(\OO_Y^{\oplus I} \twoheadrightarrow \F|_Y\) with \(I\) finite form a
  finite generating family.
",
  "lean_source": "theorem gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi.{v', u'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C]
    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}}
    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) :
    (∃ σ : M.GeneratingSections, σ.IsFiniteType) ↔
      (∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (π : SheafOfModules.free I ⟶ M), Epi π) := by
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨σ, hσ⟩
    exact ⟨σ.I, hσ.finite, σ.π, σ.epi⟩
  · rintro ⟨I, hI, π, hπ⟩
    refine ⟨{ I := I, s := M.freeHomEquiv π, epi := ?_ }, ⟨hI⟩⟩
    rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
    exact hπ",
  "proof_size_lean": 660,
  "has_sorry": false,
  "tex_file": "chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex",
  "lean_file": "AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean",
  "dep_count": 0,
  "rdep_count": 1,
  "descendant_count": 3,
  "proof_size_tex": 568,
  "effort_local": 0,
  "proof_size_tex_total": 568,
  "proof_size_lean_total": 660,
  "effort_total": 0
}

Excellent! Now I have the full picture for `lem:gf_finite_gen_iff_free_epi`:
- `proved: false` in leandag (agrees with my Python analysis)
- `has_sorry: false` - no sorry in Lean code
- `lean_source` shows a complete Lean proof (not a sorry)

Wait, `proved: false` but `has_sorry: false` and there's a complete Lean proof... This is confusing. 

Oh! I think `proved: false` in leandag means "NOT marked as proved in the blueprint" (i.e., no `\leanok`), NOT that the Lean code isn't proved. 

So the Lean code is actually complete with no sorry, but it's missing the `\leanok` marker in the blueprint. That would explain the discrepancy. Now I need to figure out whether leandag's "465 proved" count is based on `\leanok` markers or on the absence of sorry statements.

noncomputable def tautologicalQuotientComponent (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).obj
        (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).homEquiv _ _
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ((theGlueData d r).ι I) (Fin r)).hom ≫
      chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2)

/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`. Since `universalQuotient` is the descent
equalizer of pushforwards, the morphism is `equalizer.lift` of the per-chart adjoint
transposes (`tautologicalQuotientComponent`); the remaining `sorry` is the equalizing
condition — the chart compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`,
whose matrix content is `X^J = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` /
`imageMatrix`) transported through the adjunction. -/
noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
    (bundleTransitionData d r)
    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
    (fun p => by
      -- Overlap compatibility of the chart quotients: after transposing along the
      -- pullback–pushforward adjunction of the overlap immersion, this is the chart
      -- compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`, whose matrix core
      -- is `universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` (`X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`, the `imageMatrix`
      -- identity). The module-level packaging (a rectangular analogue of
      -- `matrixEnd_pullback`/`matrixEnd_comp` for the `r → d` chart quotient plus the
      -- transpose bookkeeping of the two equalizer legs) is the remaining work — see
      -- `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian_Picard_GrassmannianQuot.lean.md`.
      sorry)

/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property

The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.

Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in

/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
